page: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. This x86_64 architecture configuration delivers 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It features no local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but includes a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark testing, the r6in.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50%. Memory performance is strong, with large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks ranking in the top 25%. The instance also achieves strong results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal design eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the utility of the 1024.0 GB RAM allocation and 200 Gbps network interface. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires external EBS volumes, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for storage-heavy workloads. This server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded enterprise applications that require high network throughput and substantial memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores and 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating at 3.5 GHz, this x86_64 platform lacks local storage but provides a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark evaluations, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for PassMark CPU and stress-ng multi-core tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operation speeds. Conversely, single-core performance remains average. The bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, offering high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-dense deployments. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring high network throughput.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring 128 vCPUs, one terabyte of RAM, and high-speed 200 Gbps network throughput.

1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
